message:
docs(plan): add condensed plan variant for context-constrained reviews

Add PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI__CONDENSED.md (8,430 lines), a structural
summary of the full plan (22,000+ lines) designed for LLM review sessions
where context budget is limited.

## Purpose

The condensed variant enables:
- **Context-Constrained Review**: Other LLMs can analyze the full plan
  structure without exhausting their context window. A reviewer model
  can read the condensed version, understand the architecture, and provide
  targeted feedback on specific sections.

- **Rapid Navigation**: Engineers can quickly locate relevant sections
  without scrolling through thousands of lines of implementation code.

- **Structural Validation**: Verify the plan covers all necessary topics
  by scanning section headers and metadata summaries.

## Condensation Method

The condensed file preserves:
- All section headers and hierarchical structure (37+ sections)
- All prose descriptions and design rationale
- All tables, lists, and non-code content
- CLI command examples (extracted and summarized)

The condensed file replaces code blocks with metadata summaries:

```
[Code block omitted: Rust example code (types/logic).]
- Block length: 45 line(s).
- Counts: 2 struct(s), 1 enum(s), 0 trait(s), 1 impl(s), 3 fn(s).
- Struct: SkillPack
- Struct: PackConstraints
- Enum: CompileTarget
- Function/method: compile
- Function/method: validate
```

This preserves the semantic structure (what types exist, what functions
are defined) without consuming tokens on implementation details.

## File Size Comparison

| File | Lines | Tokens (est.) | Use Case |
|------|-------|---------------|----------|
| Full plan | ~23,000 | ~150,000 | Implementation reference |
| Condensed | ~8,430 | ~50,000 | Architecture review |

The condensed version is ~37% the size of the full plan, enabling it to
fit within a single LLM context window for comprehensive review.

## Contents Coverage

The condensed file covers all 37+ sections:
- Section 0: Background (CASS, xf, Agent Mail, NTM)
- Sections 1-3: Vision, directory structure, data models
- Sections 4-6: CLI interface, CASS integration, mining loop
- Sections 7-9: Evidence, search, bundles
- Sections 10-11: Prerequisites, phased roadmap
- Section 12: Dependencies (Cargo.toml)
- Sections 13-15: Build/test patterns, publishing, hooks
- Sections 16-18: Markdown rendering, config, API spec
- Sections 19-20: Safety filters, multi-agent coordination
- Sections 21-22: Effectiveness loop, uncertainty resolution
- Sections 23-27: Disclosure protocol, naming, version tracking, CASS patterns
- Section 28: Brenner methodology (inner truth extraction)
- Section 29: APR (Iterative refinement loop)
- Section 30: Performance profiling patterns
- Section 31: Optimization patterns
- Section 32: Security vulnerability assessment
- Section 33: Error handling patterns
- Section 34: Testing patterns
- Section 35: CI/CD automation
- Section 36: Caching and memoization
- Section 37: Debugging workflows

## Usage

```bash
# Quick structural review
head -500 PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI__CONDENSED.md

# Find all struct definitions
grep -n "Struct:" PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI__CONDENSED.md

# Find specific section
grep -n "## Section 32" PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI__CONDENSED.md

# Feed to LLM for architectural review
cat PLAN_TO_MAKE_METASKILL_CLI__CONDENSED.md | llm "Review this plan..."
```

## Maintenance

The condensed file should be regenerated when significant structural
changes are made to the full plan. A future `ms condense` command could
automate this transformation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
